svg.text: 01-Classic Mode: Classic Mode vs Beyond Mode

g.text: Allows players to use any characters they own
in Teyvat to enter levels for gameplay.
Players bring multiple characters retaining all
Only supports players using Manekins to enter
All data can be defined by the creator

svg.text: III. Faction System in Classic Mode

g.text: Compared to Beyond Mode, factions in Classic Mode are subject to certain restrictions:
• All players and their characters belong to the Player Faction (cannot be modified).
• All creations belong to the Creation Faction (cannot be modified).
• Objects → Player Faction, Creation Faction, Object Faction 1, 2, or 3.
• No dynamic faction changes; modification/event nodes cannot be used.
The relationships between factions in Classic Mode are as follows:
Rows represent the initiating party, columns represent the receiving party.
Object Faction 3 is typically used for objects friendly to the Player Faction,
assisting characters in attacking constructs. Object Faction 1 is used for regular objects.
Object Faction 2 is generally used for objects hostile to Object Faction 1.
Content not mentioned in the differences above is available in both Beyond Mode and Classic Mode,
unless otherwise specified in the comprehensive guide.
In Classic Mode, all entities cannot dynamically change factions. Faction modification-related
execution and event nodes cannot be used. New factions cannot be created.
Players use their own characters with full Teyvat data (attributes, skills, talents, weapons,
artifacts, and constellations). The creator (Craftsperson) cannot modify this data.
